mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate declines signal monetary easing that reduces discount rates applied to equity valuations. A sustained decline in FEDFUNDS over the prior 3 months produces positive S&P 500 forward returns within 20 trading days as market reprices equity risk premiums downward.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 4.24% on 2007-12-01 reflects recent Fed easing from prior peaks
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or mean 20-day forward returns during periods of declining FEDFUNDS are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity. When UNRATE increases by more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months, S&P 500 forward returns decline over the next 20 trading days due to earnings forecast downgrades and recession risk premium expansion.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 5.0% on 2007-12-01 reflects labor market softening
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 65% of triggered instances despite rising unemployment signal, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts measured by HOUST decline sharply, signaling reduced construction demand and consumer wealth destruction through home equity losses. This triggers a 60-day forward negative return signal as the market reprices growth expectations and financial sector stress materializes.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1187.0 thousand units on 2007-11-01 reflects housing market weakness
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite housing decline signal, or correlation between HOUST changes and 60-day forward returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth decelerates, measured by INDPRO, signaling weakening manufacturing activity and supply-side constraints easing. When INDPRO growth falls below 0.5% month-over-month, equity returns compress over 5 trading days as investors reduce cyclical exposure and repricing occurs.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 113.9019 on 2007-11-01 reflects industrial weakness
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are not significantly negative when INDPRO growth falls below 0.5% (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.01 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales measured by RSAFS and employment measured by PAYEMS jointly decline, signaling synchronized demand and income destruction. This interaction-driven mechanism predicts negative 20-day returns as both consumer purchasing power and employment growth deteriorate simultaneously, amplifying recession risk perception.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS declines > 1% month-over-month AND PAYEMS growth < 0.1% month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 382928.0 million on 2007-12-01 and PAYEMS at 138495.0 thousand on 2007-12-01
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of instances where both RSAFS and PAYEMS decline simultaneously, or interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.05) in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation measured by CPIAUCSL accelerates while FEDFUNDS remains below 5%, creating a negative real interest rate regime. This regime-conditional mechanism predicts positive equity returns over 60 days as investors flee cash and seek inflation hedges, but reverses to negative returns if FEDFUNDS rises above 5% to combat inflation.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 210.565 on 2007-11-01 and FEDFUNDS at 4.24% on 2007-12-01
regime_hint: negative real rates with inflation acceleration
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are not significantly positive when real rates are negative (t-test p > 0.05), or the mechanism reverses direction when FEDFUNDS exceeds 5% in fewer than 70% of test instances.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment measured by PAYEMS decelerates sharply, signaling labor market deterioration that precedes broader economic contraction. When PAYEMS growth falls below 0.05% month-over-month, S&P 500 forward returns decline over 60 trading days as the market reprices recession probability and earnings growth expectations.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 138495.0 thousand on 2007-12-01 reflects labor market softening
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are not significantly negative when PAYEMS growth falls below 0.05% (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP measured by GDPC1 growth decelerates below 2% annualized rate while housing starts measured by HOUST decline simultaneously, signaling synchronized demand destruction across residential and overall economic activity. This interaction-driven mechanism predicts negative 20-day returns as recession risk premium expands.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 annualized growth < 2% AND HOUST declines > 10% from prior quarter
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 11658.9 billion on 2007-07-01 and HOUST at 1187.0 thousand on 2007-11-01
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 65% of instances where both GDPC1 growth and HOUST decline simultaneously, or interaction term is not statistically significant (p > 0.05) in multivariate regression.
